channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: 'Make it legal next time': Another blistering 100m for Aussie Nketia
item.description: For the second time this year, Eddie Nketia stops the clock in what would be an Australian record for the 100m, but is aided by an illegal tailwind.

media:group.media:description: Edward Nketia won the 100m in 9.74 seconds with an illegal tailwind of 5.6 metres per second.

item.title: How an Australian designer's fashion fame spurred a mental health unravelling
item.description: Rochelle Gregory has clothed everyone from Paris Hilton to Dua Lipa. Now the designer is using film to candidly explore her mental health battle while showcasing her latest collection.

media:group.media:description: Rochelle Gregory, who runs fashion label Rocky Rafaela, has laid bare her mental health battle in a short film screened during her latest runway show.

item.title: Breaking: Man found dead after fire at block of units in Ballarat's Wendouree
item.description: The man was pulled from the house by emergency crews, but could not be revived.
item.dc:creator: Laura Mayers and Coco Veldkamp

media:group.media:description: Police are investigating a fatal house fire in the Ballarat suburb of Wendouree this morning.

item.title: Bid to reopen Albany hydrotherapy pool rejected
item.description: The WA health minister rejects recommissioning Albany's public hydrotherapy pool despite a feasibility study saying there is a justifiable cause to do so.
item.dc:creator: Atlas Cook

media:group.media:description: Glenys Nottle is one of 100 people who would make use of the hydrotherapy pool.

item.title: Pilots parachute from jets after mid-flight collision at US air show
item.description: Two fighter jets have collided mid-air during an air show at a US Air Force base, forcing pilots to eject and parachute to safety.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: The two Growlers collided in mid-air, prompting the crew to bail out over Mountain Home Air Force Base.

item.title: 1.2 million Queensland homes without compliant smoke alarms near deadline
item.description: A national company estimates many Queensland homes are unprepared as the deadline to install compliant smoke alarms nears.
item.dc:creator: Sally Rafferty

media:group.media:description: Brandon Lewis lost everything in a house fire in 2014.

item.title: Live: ASX set to fall after sharp drops on Wall Street, AUD also lower
item.description: Australian markets are set to begin the week in negative territory, with ASX 200 futures pointing to a drop of around 0.4 per cent on Monday after both US indexes fell sharply from record highs. Follow live.

media:group.media:description: Another day of losses is expected on the ASX 200.

item.title: Live: Plibersek denies tax on discretionary trusts amounts to 'death tax'
item.description: Social Services Minister Tanya Plibersek has denied that the government's tax on discretionary trusts amounts to a "death tax". Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini

media:group.media:description: Australian Environment Minister Tanya Plibersek speaks during a press conference at Parliament House in Canberra, Monday, 27 November, 2023. (AAP Image/Lukas Coch) NO ARCHIVING

item.title: Live: No Walsh for Queensland as Blues coach accidentally lists former Maroon
item.description: James Tedesco wins back his NSW fullback spot in a State of Origin I squad featuring seven potential debutants, with coach Laurie Daley accidentally announcing the name of an ex-Queensland player. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Nick Campton

media:group.media:description: Casey McGuire (centre) played two Origins in 2005.

item.title: Kittens turned away from shelters amid 'heartbreaking' stray cat crisis
item.description: Animal shelters across New South Wales sound the alarm on a worsening stray cat crisis.
item.dc:creator: Ben Clifford and Courtney Yeandle

media:group.media:description: Animal rescuers across New South Wales say they are facing a stray cat "crisis".

item.title: Meet the women rangers in Central Australia working to protect country
item.description: Dozens of Central Land Council Aboriginal rangers gathered east of Alice Springs last week for the 2026 Women's Ranger Camp — a time for health, learning and healing in the wake of the town's recent loss of Kumanjayi Little Baby.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange

media:group.media:description: Aboriginal women rangers hiking into N'Dhala Gorge on Eastern Arrernte country. Taken in Central Australia (NT), May 2026.

item.title: Advocates say more women being coerced and threatened to leave Australia
item.description: Advocates for migrant women say they're seeing more cases of exit trafficking — where an offender uses coercion, threats or deception to organise a person's departure from Australia.
item.dc:creator: Tahlia Roy

media:group.media:description: Rupali did not know what exit trafficking was when she was deceived.

item.title: Criminal cases at risk of being halted as NT legal aid crisis worsens
item.description: Lawyers in the Northern Territory are commencing a landmark legal challenge that could require courts to halt some cases due to defendants being unable to access a lawyer amid a worsening legal aid crisis.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Clancy Dane is pursuing the legal challenge on behalf of a client who was refused legal aid.

item.title: Late bogey costs Cameron Smith after PGA Championship surge
item.description: Australian Cameron Smith makes a hot start to the final round at the PGA Championship, putting himself right in the mix to end his major drought, before tripping up on the penultimate hole.

media:group.media:description: Cameron Smith did not have any bogeys through the front nine.

item.title: French Epstein probe sees 10 new alleged victims come forward
item.description: About 10 new suspected victims have come forward as part of a French probe into the network of late US sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

media:group.media:description: French prosecutors are investigating the network of late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Elk have been spotted in the wild in SA. Where did they come from?
item.description: A South Australian landowner has spotted elk on her property, outside containment lines of deer farms. There are no known established wild elk herds in this state.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Adamo

media:group.media:description: Anne Daw was surprised to find elk on her property.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: New front emerges as UAE strikes back
item.description: The UAE shifts from target to combatant amid reports the US and Israel are preparing to renew strikes on Iran, writes Matthew Doran.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: ABC Middle East correspondent Matthew Doran in Jerusalem

item.title: New foreshore plan for historic Sydney park revealed
item.description: A new foreshore plan for Callan Park in Sydney's inner west includes a cafe, but the future of commercial opportunities at the site depends now on the passing of amended legislation.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross

media:group.media:description: The future of commercial opportunities at Callan Park depends now on the passing of amended legislation.

item.title: Students of newly merged Adelaide University protest changing of graduation venue
item.description: The newly merged Adelaide University has changed the venue of its graduation ceremonies from Bonython Hall, to Pridham Hall with students launching a petition in opposition.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maunder

media:group.media:description: Students at the newly merged Adelaide University say they’re disappointed that Bonython Hall will no longer be the venue of their graduation ceremonies.

item.title: Steel and lead are facing trial by fire in SA's regional cities
item.description: The South Australian government has put all its chips on the table for the Whyalla steelworks and Port Pirie lead smelter. The next six months will be critical.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Kelsall

media:group.media:description: Whyalla's blast furnace has faced ongoing problems.

item.title: More rain forecast for south-east Queensland before temperatures plummet
item.description: Coastal rain will linger today and tomorrow, before clear skies and and chilly nights arrive on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Dye

media:group.media:description: Gloomy weather at Burleigh Heads on Sunday. The forecast improves on Wednesday.

item.title: Sobering-up centre to close less than four years after public intoxication reforms
item.description: A 20-bed facility in the Melbourne suburb of Collingwood will close in 2027 after operating for just a few years following legal reforms.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Eddie

media:group.media:description: After Victoria decriminalised public intoxication in 2023, it established the Collingwood sobering up centre.

item.title: Qld developer donation overhaul yields $170,000 in cash
item.description: More than $170,000 in donations from developers has been given to Queensland state politicians since laws banning developer donations were overturned in March.
item.dc:creator: Liam Walsh

media:group.media:description: Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie oversees state government agency Economic Development Queensland.

item.title: The dilemma Wallabies and All Blacks face when players head overseas
item.description: Both Australia and New Zealand strive to protect Super Rugby Pacific as their premier domestic competition, even it if it has consequences for the Wallabies and All Blacks.
item.dc:creator: Luke Pentony

media:group.media:description: Australia's Len Ikitau (left) is playing for Exeter in the English premiership.

item.title: Melbourne nightclub has operated for two years without insurance
item.description: Pride of our Footscray stopped paying public liability insurance when 18 insurers refused to underwrite the venue. The manager says operating without cover keeps staff on tenterhooks.
item.dc:creator: Andi Yu

media:group.media:description: HollyPop hosts drag trivia at a gay nightclub in Melbourne's west.
